meta.notes: Founder Story reel due Week 4 (last featured Week 2 — must appear at least once every 2 weeks under the 2-reel budget). This week adds to avoid list for future runs: one-leg balance test, sleep + muscle protein synthesis, Fitovo readiness scoring feature reveal. Previous weeks' avoid list observed: VO2max decline, sarcopenia/muscle loss intro, Norwegian 4x4, dead hang/split squat/hip hinge, HRV basics, Zone 2 training, active couch potato/sitting paradox, sit-to-stand test, non-functional overreaching, zone training carousel, 3-workout week carousel. NOTE: Previous week files had 10-15 hashtags (violating the 3-5 rule) and weekend Stories (violating the weekday-only rule). Both corrected this week.

reels.hook: Stand on one leg. Close your eyes. Set a timer. What you find out in the next 10 seconds is worth knowing.
reels.script:
Stand on one leg. Close your eyes. Set a timer.

If you can't hold 10 seconds — that number is worth knowing.

A study in the British Journal of Sports Medicine followed 1,700 adults and found those who failed this test had an 84% higher risk of dying in the following decade.

Balance reveals hip strength, core stability, and the neurological coordination that keeps you independent long-term.

The good news: every one of those qualities is trainable.

Three exercises that build it: single-leg deadlift, slow step-up, heel-to-toe walking. Under 10 minutes combined.

Save this — progression guide coming this week.
reels.cta: Save this — progression guide coming this week.
reels.caption:
Stand on one leg. Close your eyes. Set a timer.

If you can't hold 10 seconds — that number is worth paying attention to.

A study in the British Journal of Sports Medicine followed over 1,700 adults and found those who failed this simple test had an 84% higher risk of dying in the following decade.

Not because balance is everything — but because balance reflects hip strength, core stability, and the neurological pathways that keep you functional and independent long-term.

The good news: every one of those qualities is trainable.

Start here:
→ Single-leg deadlift
→ Slow step-up
→ Heel-to-toe walking

10 minutes a day. Progressive over time.

Save this 🔖 — progression guide coming this week.
reels.tiktok_caption: stand on one leg, close your eyes, set a timer. under 10 seconds? that number matters. 1,700-person study linked balance to decade-long health risk. 3 exercises fix it — save this 🦵 #fitnessafter40 #longevity
reels.visual_notes: Direct-to-camera, medium close-up. Open with avatar glancing briefly downward (as if lifting a foot) before returning to camera — a subtle physical cue on 'stand on one leg'. Measured, warm delivery throughout. Pause after '84% higher risk of dying in the following decade' — let the number breathe for a full beat. Soften visibly and smile on 'the good news' pivot. The three exercise names can appear as clean text overlays as they're spoken. No gym background needed — kitchen or home office keeps it accessible. 'Save this' should feel like advice from a trusted friend, not a sales prompt.

reels.hook: You can train consistently and still wonder why you're not recovering. Here's what most people miss.
reels.script:
You can train consistently and still wonder why you're not recovering. Here's what most people miss.

During deep sleep, your body releases growth hormone — the primary driver of muscle repair. After 40, that release decreases. Poor sleep doesn't just make you tired — it suppresses recovery directly.

Research suggests poor sleep can reduce muscle protein synthesis by up to 18% overnight.

Training on bad sleep is like filling a bucket with a hole in it.

The most underrated training variable after 40? Your sleep.

Drop a 1 to 5 in the comments — how's yours?
reels.cta: Drop a 1 to 5 in the comments — how's your sleep right now?
reels.caption:
You're training consistently.
Showing up.
Doing the work.

And still wondering why the recovery isn't matching the effort.

Here's what most programmes don't tell you.

During deep sleep, your body releases growth hormone — the primary signal for overnight muscle repair and adaptation. After 40, this release naturally decreases. And poor sleep doesn't just make you tired — it directly suppresses the recovery process your training depends on.

Research suggests poor sleep can reduce muscle protein synthesis by up to 18% overnight.

That means training on bad sleep is like filling a leaky bucket. You keep pouring in the effort — but the output won't match.

The most underrated training variable after 40 isn't a supplement, a protocol, or a piece of kit.

It's protecting your sleep.

What's your sleep like right now? 1 = terrible, 5 = excellent — drop your number below 👇
reels.tiktok_caption: training consistently but not recovering? this is probably why. after 40, poor sleep can cut overnight muscle protein synthesis by up to 18%. training on bad sleep = filling a leaky bucket. drop your sleep score 1-5 below 👇 #fitnessafter40 #sleepscience
reels.visual_notes: Direct-to-camera, intimate and reflective — closer to a quiet conversation than a high-energy reel. Home setting, not a gym. Start with a slightly slower, more considered delivery than usual — this topic earns it. Lean forward subtly on 'here's what most people miss.' Consider a simple graphic at the growth hormone mention: a sleep/moon icon next to a muscle icon with a downward arrow, labelled 'after 40'. Pause meaningfully after 'filling a bucket with a hole in it' — let the visual metaphor land. End with genuine, unhurried curiosity on the question — interested, not performative.

carousels.title: What Fitovo actually does — in plain English
slides: What Fitovo actually does — in plain English →
Every morning, it reads three signals from your Apple Watch: HRV, resting heart rate, sleep quality.
From those three signals, it calculates one thing: your readiness score for the day.
Score high: your nervous system is recovered. Today is a push day.
Score moderate: partial recovery. Fitovo schedules a lighter session — still progress.
Score low: your body is still adapting. Walk, mobilise, or rest. No guilt.
Most apps send you the same workout regardless of how you slept or recovered. Fitovo doesn't.
The best training outcome isn't more sessions — it's the right session at the right time. Waitlist → link in bio.
carousels.caption:
Most fitness apps have one setting: go.

Fitovo has three.

Swipe to see how readiness-based training works — and why it matters more after 40 than any programme design. 👉

This is the feature I'm most excited about building.

Waitlist is open — link in bio. Early access spots are limited.
carousels.gamma_prompt: Create a clean, minimal 8-slide carousel called 'What Fitovo actually does — in plain English.' Visual style: white or very light grey background (#F7F7F5), bold near-black primary type (#1A1A1A), single green accent colour (#3D9B6A) used sparingly on numbers, arrows, and key callout words only. Sans-serif typeface (Inter or DM Sans). No stock photography. No gradients. Overall feel: trusted editorial product explainer. Slide 1: full-bleed bold title slide — 'What Fitovo actually does — in plain English →'. Largest font on the page. Swipe arrow bottom-right. Slide 2: body text only — 'Every morning, it reads three signals from your Apple Watch: HRV, resting heart rate, sleep quality.' Slide 3: 'From those three signals, it calculates one thing: your readiness score for the day.' Slide 4: add a small green filled circle top-left as a visual marker — 'Score high: your nervous system is recovered. Today is a push day.' Slide 5: add a small amber/yellow filled circle top-left — 'Score moderate: partial recovery. Fitovo schedules a lighter session — still progress.' Slide 6: add a small red filled circle top-left — 'Score low: your body is still adapting. Walk, mobilise, or rest. No guilt.' Slide 7: 'Most apps send you the same workout regardless of how you slept or recovered. Fitovo doesn't.' — bold the word 'doesn't' in green. Slide 8: 'The best training outcome isn't more sessions — it's the right session at the right time. Waitlist → link in bio.' — bold 'right session at the right time' in green. Keep all slide body text under 20 words. Add a small 'fitovo' wordmark bottom-right on every slide.

stories.concept: Question sticker Story. White background with bold green text overlay: 'What's one thing you want your body to handle better this week?' Add Instagram Question sticker below with prompt: 'Drop your intention here ↓'. Post 8–9am. No video, no editing — background colour, text overlay, sticker, post. Under 2 minutes.

Re-share Tuesday's Reel directly to Stories via the paper-plane icon → 'Add to Your Story'. Add bold text sticker top-left: 'NEW ↑'. Add a second smaller text sticker below: 'Can you balance on one leg for 10 seconds with eyes closed? Try it first.' Two stickers, one reshare, no other edits. Under 2 minutes.

Quick tip text overlay Story. Dark near-black background. One large bold white text line centred on screen: 'Poor sleep doesn't just make you tired — it cuts overnight muscle repair.' Smaller green sub-text below: 'Protect your sleep like it's part of the training plan.' No video, no B-roll. Type the text, choose background colour, post. Under 2 minutes.

Poll Story. Plain light background. Bold text overlay at top: 'How well are you sleeping right now — honestly?' Add Instagram Poll sticker with 4 options: A) Solid — 7-8 hrs / B) Decent but broken / C) 5-6 hrs and feel it / D) Sleep is a mess. Post any time Thursday. Under 2 minutes.

Re-share Friday's Reel to Stories after it goes live. Add bold text sticker top-left: 'NEW ↑'. Add Instagram Question sticker below the Reel preview with prompt: 'What's your win this week? Training, sleep, energy — drop it below 👇'. Two stickers, one reshare, no other edits. Under 2 minutes.
